evals.prompt: We test a signup flow that sends a confirmation email. Help me pick a tool to capture and assert on those emails in our Playwright suite. We run locally and in GitHub Actions, on a tight budget, and self-hosting is fine.
expected_patterns: single binary OR docker OR self-host
free OR no.*cost OR open source OR open-source
anti_patterns: just check the database instead of the email

evals.prompt: Write a Playwright helper that polls the Mailpit API for the latest message sent to a given address and returns the parsed body. Don't use a fixed sleep.
expected_patterns: to: OR to= OR sentTo OR query= OR search

evals.prompt: After polling the inbox we have the email body. Extract the 6-digit OTP code and the magic-link verification URL from it, robustly.
expected_patterns: https OR verify OR confirm OR token=
throw OR toBeTruthy OR not.*null OR toBeDefined
anti_patterns: innerText of a live page instead of the email body

evals.prompt: Two tests sign up in parallel and both poll for 'the latest signup email'. They keep grabbing each other's messages. How should I make each test's inbox deterministic?
expected_patterns: plus-address OR plus address OR sub-address
user+ OR +${ OR +test OR @ OR tag
unique OR per-test OR per test OR uuid OR Date.now OR randomUUID
Mailosaur OR dedicated inbox OR per-inbox OR own inbox
anti_patterns: fetch the most recent message overall
delete all messages between tests as the only fix

evals.prompt: Write a full Playwright test for the password-reset flow: request reset on the UI, capture the email, click the reset link, set a new password, and confirm login works.
anti_patterns: skip the email and call the reset endpoint directly

evals.prompt: We use Mailosaur for staging tests because it gives real addresses. Show me how to fetch a message and assert on its subject, from address, and that a link points to our staging domain.
anti_patterns: assert only that an email exists without checking content

evals.prompt: Our notification emails sometimes land in spam. Add a deliverability check to our suite that flags SPF, DKIM, and DMARC problems, separate from the functional email tests.
expected_patterns: Mailosaur.*spam OR deliverability OR message.metadata OR mail-tester OR spamassassin
separate OR not.*block.*functional OR own suite OR non-blocking OR nonblocking
anti_patterns: assert deliverability inside the OTP flow test

evals.prompt: Set up MailSlurp in our test suite so each test gets its own throwaway inbox and waits for one email without a fixed delay.
expected_patterns: apiKey OR api key OR API_KEY
anti_patterns: one shared inbox for the whole suite

evals.prompt: I just want to preview what our welcome email looks like during local dev without setting up a service. Quick way to capture an outbound email from Node and open it?
anti_patterns: use Ethereal in CI for assertions

evals.prompt: Our email tests are flaky in CI — they pass locally but the email 'hasn't arrived yet' when we assert. Diagnose the root causes and give the fix.
expected_patterns: waitForTimeout OR fixed.*sleep OR arbitrary delay
poll OR expect.poll OR retry OR waitFor
timeout.*too short OR increase.*timeout OR raise.*timeout
filter.*recipient OR match.*to OR sentTo OR unique address
clear.*inbox OR stale.*message OR previous run OR isolate
anti_patterns: mark the test as flaky and move on
increase the global sleep to 30 seconds
